name: North Carolina Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
renewalCycle: Annual (by March 31) + 30 CE hours per 2 years

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Must show a master's or doctoral degree in nutrition or a related field from a regionally accredited institution.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of current, active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (for Category A applicants).

requiredDocuments.name: Government-Issued Photo ID
requiredDocuments.description: A clear copy of a valid, government-issued identification.

requiredDocuments.name: Criminal Background Check Authorization
requiredDocuments.description: Completed authorization form and associated $38 fee.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a North Carolina dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost for a Category A (RDN) license is $248, which comprises a $60 application fee, a $150 issuance fee, and a $38 criminal background check fee. Annual renewal is $95.

mainEntity.name: When does the North Carolina dietitian license expire?

acceptedAnswer.text: All North Carolina dietitian/nutritionist licenses expire on March 31st each year, regardless of when they were issued. Annual renewal must be submitted between January 1st and March 31st.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for NC dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Licensees must complete 30 hours of continuing education every two years. For RDNs (Category A), meeting the CDR's continuing education standards (75 hours per 5-year cycle) fulfills the state requirement.

mainEntity.name: Does North Carolina have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in North Carolina. To work in North Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to North Carolina?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 3 Business Days.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
